remember: Students will be able to recall the key features of contemporary security challenges and their impact on international relations.
Students will be able to identify the major types of security threats and their causes.
Students will be able to list the various actors involved in the global security landscape.
understand: Students will be able to comprehend the concept of security in the contemporary world and its relevance to national and international security.
Students will be able to analyze the relationship between economic and environmental security and other types of security.
Students will be able to understand the role of non-state actors in the contemporary security scenario.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concepts of security to real-world scenarios and case studies.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of security policies and strategies on national and international security.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of different security measures and their outcomes.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the root causes of contemporary security challenges and their relationship with globalization and urbanization.
Students will be able to compare and contrast different approaches to security (e.g., military, economic, environmental) and their implications.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of international organizations and institutions in promoting global security.

ncert_objectives: To understand the concept of security in the contemporary world and its various dimensions.
To analyze the impact of globalization and urbanization on security challenges.
To evaluate the role of non-state actors in the contemporary security scenario.
board_exam_objectives: Explain the concept of security in the contemporary world.
Describe the major types of security threats and their causes.
Discuss the role of international organizations in promoting global security.
